name: Apify browser automation
maintenance_status: Active. Apify maintains the platform, Actor runtime, JavaScript and Python SDKs, Crawlee, storage, proxy, standby, and security documentation, with current platform and repository activity after January 2025.
license: Managed commercial platform under Apify terms. Apify SDK for Python and Crawlee for Python are Apache-2.0, which is permissive for use by AGPL-3.0 ApplyPilot with notices preserved. Community Store Actors have independent licenses and terms that must be reviewed individually.
pricing_model: Subscription credits plus usage. Free provides $5/month of prepaid usage; Starter is $29/month with $29 usage; Scale is $199/month with $199 usage; Business is $999/month with $999 usage. Current compute is $0.20/CU on Free/Starter, $0.16 on Scale, and $0.13 on Business; 1 CU equals 1 GB RAM for 1 hour. Transfer, storage operations, residential proxy traffic, rented Actors, and Store event charges can add cost.
marginal_cost_per_attempt: [uncertain] A custom browser Actor using 2-4 GB RAM for 3-8 minutes consumes about 0.10-0.53 CU, or about $0.02-$0.11 on Free/Starter before transfer, storage, proxy, Store, retry, and model charges. Larger browser images, many short cold runs, residential traffic at $8/GB, or paid Store Actors can dominate. Measure actual run usage rather than treating this range as a quote.
projected_cost_per_verified_apply: [uncertain] ApplyPilot's 2026-07-09 baseline is 909 verified applies from 6,371 terminal attempts at $2.0405 all-in. If each terminal attempt added an illustrative $0.05 Apify browser run and completion stayed unchanged, cost would mechanically rise about $0.35 to roughly $2.39 per verified apply before model, proxy, Store, and human effects. Apify must lower failures or replace current agent cost to produce savings; no ATS benchmark proves that.
billing_granularity: Compute units are memory multiplied by duration with one-second minimum granularity. Subscription credits expire each billing cycle; paying plans continue into overage up to configured limits. Separate billing can include external/internal transfer, storage GB-hours and operations, residential proxy GB, datacenter IPs, Store rental, or pay-per-event Actor charges.
model_cost: No model is required for deterministic Playwright/Crawlee Actors. AI Actors, LLM extraction, or ApplyPilot's answerer/verifier add provider-specific token or event costs, and third-party Store Actors may charge per run, result, event, or rental. Meter model usage outside CU.
infrastructure_cost: Apify hosts Docker Actor builds, CPU/RAM, browser processes, APIs, schedules, queues, datasets, key-value stores, request queues, proxies, logs, and standby instances. Cost is primarily CU plus transfer/proxy/storage; standby keeps capacity warm and therefore continues consuming compute while ready.
human_recovery_cost: [uncertain] Apify Console logs and storage help diagnosis, but the core Actor platform does not provide a universal job-application Live View/owner-takeover flow comparable to browser-specialist vendors. Login, OTP, and ambiguous submission may require a custom remote-view channel or separate local browser, so operator time is unmeasured and potentially high.
adapter_maintenance_tco: [uncertain] Medium to high. Apify provides reusable Crawlee and Actor primitives but ApplyPilot must package browser dependencies, maintain ATS logic, design Actor inputs/outputs, secure candidate data, map run state into fleet events, control storage retention, and decide whether third-party Actors are trustworthy and stable.
verified_completion_rate: [uncertain] Apify publishes no verified job-application completion rate stratified by ATS. Store ratings or scraping success are not positive submission evidence. Benchmark custom Actors against ApplyPilot's 14.27% mixed baseline with identical jobs and verification rules.
failure_modes: Actor build, image, dependency, startup, migration, timeout, abort, or memory failure.
Browser crash, Playwright selector drift, navigation timeout, or page resource overload.
Proxy block, CAPTCHA, authentication, OTP, or target-site policy denial.
Community Actor behavior, pricing, schema, or maintenance can change independently.
Required ATS fields, uploads, custom widgets, and sensitive questions may remain unsupported.
A run can succeed technically while application submission is absent or ambiguous.
crash_recovery: [uncertain] Actor runs expose lifecycle states, logs, storage, migration/restart behavior, request queues, retries, and persisted datasets/key-value stores. ApplyPilot can implement checkpointed resume, but arbitrary browser page state and exactly-once final submit are not automatically recovered after container or browser failure.
exactly_once_submit_safety: Not native. Actor run IDs and request queues help deduplicate work, but ApplyPilot must prevent duplicate applications with job-level keys, pre-submit checkpoints, one irreversible action, network/DOM confirmation, and crash-uncertain quarantine.
verification_evidence_tier: Custom Actors can store ATS IDs, response payloads, confirmation DOM, URLs, screenshots, logs, and datasets. Delayed email remains external. Evidence quality depends entirely on Actor implementation; a SUCCEEDED Actor status is not evidence that an application was submitted.
verification_latency: [uncertain] Run logs and outputs are available during or after execution, while email/account reconciliation is delayed. Short Actors pay cold-start overhead and standby can reduce it at ongoing compute cost. Measure submission-to-output and delayed evidence separately.
headless_headed_parity: [uncertain] Apify browser Actors normally run headless Chromium in containers; local development can be headed, but production parity with a user's browser profile, fingerprint, permissions, extensions, and network is not guaranteed. Test each ATS and auth route.
python_integration: Strong. Apify has official Python SDK and API client support, Crawlee for Python, Python Actor templates, and Playwright-based crawlers. ApplyPilot can call Actors over API or package portions of its Python runtime into an Actor.
playwright_cdp_reuse: Partial. Existing Playwright Python code can run inside an Apify Actor or be adapted to Crawlee, but Apify is not primarily a remote-CDP endpoint for attaching current ApplyPilot adapters to a managed browser. Existing local Chrome ports and profiles do not transfer automatically.
migration_effort: Medium to large. A custom Actor requires Docker/build configuration, input and output schemas, secrets, storage/retention, run orchestration, cost tags, callbacks or polling, and fleet result integration. Reusing a Store Actor reduces initial work but adds third-party trust, schema, pricing, and maintenance risk.
ats_fit: [uncertain] Technically flexible enough for Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Workable, Workday, and long-tail Chromium flows. Best fit is batch, stateless, or crawl-oriented work and reusable custom Actors; weaker fit is owner-authenticated, interactive, exactly-once submission unless ApplyPilot builds additional controls.
deterministic_coverage: [uncertain] Apify does not increase deterministic ATS coverage by itself. Crawlee can orchestrate stable selectors and request queues, and the Store may contain useful components, but coverage still comes from ApplyPilot-owned adapters and verified schemas.
escape_rate: [uncertain] Unknown. Expect fallback for account login, OTP, sensitive questions, novel widgets, policy blocks, and ambiguous submission. Proxy tooling may improve page access but must not be treated as permission to bypass controls.
control_plane_overhead: Adds Actor build/version selection, run-start API, container startup, input serialization, output storage, status polling/webhooks, and transfer between ApplyPilot and Apify. Batching jobs or Standby can reduce startup overhead but complicates isolation, billing, and exactly-once handling.
observability_joinability: Good if ApplyPilot stores Actor run ID, build, task, dataset/key-value IDs, usage, worker/route metadata, and result-event keys. APIs expose run status, resource usage, logs, and storage, allowing queue-to-container-to-cost joins; browser network evidence must be instrumented by the Actor.
persistent_profiles: [uncertain] Browser cookies and state can be stored in key-value storage or custom persisted volumes/state, but Apify does not provide a reviewed turnkey portable Chrome-profile product for ApplyPilot. Named storage persists indefinitely, creating privacy and stale-session risks; concurrent use needs application-level locking.
existing_session_attach: No reviewed native path safely attaches an Actor to an already-authenticated local ApplyPilot Chrome tab or CDP port across the network. Use an Actor-owned session or retain the local owner browser route.
login_and_otp: [uncertain] Custom Actors can read encrypted inputs or environment variables and poll external services, but owner takeover, OTP relay, MFA approval, and credential policy must be built by ApplyPilot. Avoid sending candidate credentials or OTPs to third-party Store Actors.
extension_support: [uncertain] A custom Docker Actor can in principle package Chromium extensions, but support depends on browser launch mode, container image, extension manifest, and headless behavior. Native messaging and narrowly permissioned extension workflows require a dedicated proof and security review.
session_data_boundary: Actor inputs, environment variables, browser traffic, cookies, resumes, screenshots, logs, datasets, and key-value records are processed on Apify infrastructure. Encrypted inputs and environment variables are available. Unnamed storage retention depends on plan; named storage is retained indefinitely until deleted. Personal-data processing requires an applicable DPA and explicit retention/deletion controls.
required_field_completeness: Not guaranteed by Apify or Crawlee. ApplyPilot-owned Actor logic must enumerate required controls, detect validation messages and conditional fields, and refuse an incomplete plan before submit.
answer_provenance: Not native. Keep approved profile/resume evidence and source IDs in ApplyPilot, pass only required values to the Actor, and record which approved answer populated each field. Community Actors must not invent candidate claims.
unsupported_question_fallback: Custom Actor logic should stop and emit a structured unsupported-question event for ApplyPilot's answerer or human queue. A generic retry is unsafe for sensitive or unknown application questions.
prompt_injection_resistance: Treat page and Store Actor output as untrusted. Constrain domains, inputs, Actor permissions, API tokens, outbound services, secrets, and final-submit commands. Prefer ApplyPilot-owned source over opaque community Actors for candidate credentials and irreversible actions.
site_permission_and_terms: Use only for lawful, permitted automation. Apify provides infrastructure and proxy products, but target ATS terms, access controls, robots/policy, candidate consent, and employer restrictions remain ApplyPilot's responsibility. Proxy rotation is not permission.
irreversible_action_guard: Must be implemented in the Actor and ApplyPilot controller: complete answer plan, dedup key, policy check, pre-submit snapshot, one final click/request, and authoritative confirmation. Actor retries must never repeat an ambiguous final action automatically.
concurrency: Current plan limits are 25 concurrent runs on Free, 32 on Starter, 128 on Scale, and 256 on Business, with custom enterprise limits. Actual browser concurrency also depends on RAM per run, account usage, Actor design, proxy capacity, and target-site limits.
startup_latency: [uncertain] Public docs do not provide a universal Actor cold-start SLA. New runs pass READY before RUNNING and short runs suffer repeated startup; Standby keeps HTTP Actors ready but bills their ongoing compute. Measure build-cache, run allocation, browser launch, and first-DOM latency.
warm_session_reuse: Supported through batching multiple requests in one Actor, request queues, custom persisted state, or Actor Standby. Warm reuse improves resource efficiency but requires strict per-candidate isolation, cookie locking, timeout handling, and cost controls.
tracing_and_replay: Moderate. Apify supplies run logs, status, datasets, key-value storage, screenshots if captured, request queues, and usage details. Full browser video, DOM replay, CDP network archives, and deterministic replay are not universal platform guarantees and must be added to the Actor.
deployment_modes: Managed Apify cloud Actors, scheduled/tasks/API runs, Standby HTTP Actors, and local development for Apify SDK/Crawlee projects. Crawlee and SDK code can be self-hosted outside Apify, but managed platform capabilities and billing do not transfer automatically.
browser_version_matrix: Primarily Chromium through Playwright/Puppeteer/Crawlee browser crawlers; Firefox/WebKit availability depends on custom images and framework support rather than a managed matrix. Pin browser and Playwright versions in Actor builds and test ATS compatibility before rollout.
canary_stop_loss: Use run memory/time limits, account usage caps, abort controls, max retries, queue limits, and billing notifications. ApplyPilot must add per-route CU/proxy/model ceilings, failure breakers, Store Actor version pins, and no-retry rules after ambiguous submit.
benchmark_design: Compare an ApplyPilot-owned Apify Playwright Actor with the current local route on matched ATS-stratified jobs. Hold adapters, answers, and submit policy constant; record CU, RAM, runtime, transfer, proxy bytes, storage operations, cold start, retries, evidence tier, fallback, and human minutes. Evaluate community Actors separately.
route_funnel: Eligibility and terms check -> deterministic route -> Apify canary gate -> start pinned custom Actor with minimal input -> required-field/provenance validation -> pre-submit checkpoint -> one final action -> structured evidence output -> email reconciliation -> verified, ambiguous quarantine, fallback, or reject.
step_context_budget: [uncertain] Deterministic Actors need no model context; meter Playwright actions, pages, request-queue operations, logs, screenshots, transfer bytes, and storage calls. AI Actors must additionally meter model input/output tokens, tool calls, and Store events.
cache_replay_yield: [uncertain] Long-running Actors, request queues, browser cache, and persisted state can reduce repeated work, but no ApplyPilot warm-hit or no-model replay rate exists. Reuse must be invalidated on selector, login, browser, or candidate-state change.
confidence_intervals: [uncertain] No ATS-specific intervals exist. Use Wilson intervals for completion/fallback by ATS and bootstrap cost per verified apply, with separate cold and warm arms and at least 30-50 eligible attempts per major route before expansion.
historical_bucket_impact: [uncertain] ApplyPilot has 1,894 agent/browser-runtime failures costing $310.2143 and 2,042 preflight/policy failures costing $289.3490 in the fresh report. Apify may reduce container orchestration, retry, and browser lifecycle failures or enable cheap batch preflight, but can add startup and transfer cost. It cannot be credited against expired/policy work or Workday's high cost until a route-level canary proves an effect.
recommendation: Watch, with a narrow canary only if the reusable Actor ecosystem or burst orchestration is specifically needed. Apify is a capable general execution platform, but its CU-plus-transfer-plus-proxy economics and weaker direct attach/human-takeover fit make it less compelling than a dedicated low-cost CDP browser for ApplyPilot's primary hosted-browser route.
